name: Accessibility Testing with axe-core
description: Audit React components for WCAG accessibility violations using axe-core in tests, CI, and browser DevTools.
trendingReason: Accessibility lawsuits are increasing and WCAG compliance is now a requirement for many organizations

steps.title: Install axe-core testing utilities
steps.description: Add jest-axe (or vitest-axe) to run accessibility checks in your test suite.

steps.title: Write an accessibility test
steps.description: Create a test that renders a component and checks for WCAG violations.
steps.tip: Run axe checks on every component to catch missing labels, low contrast, and incorrect ARIA attributes.

steps.title: Add eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y for static analysis
steps.description: Catch accessibility issues at lint time before they reach tests or production.

steps.title: Run a full-page accessibility audit with Lighthouse
steps.description: Use Lighthouse specifically for the accessibility category.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# React Accessibility Audit
set -euo pipefail

echo "=== Installing a11y testing tools ==="
pnpm add -D axe-core vitest-axe eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y

echo ""
echo "=== Running Lighthouse accessibility audit ==="
URL=${1:-http://localhost:3000}
npx lighthouse "$URL" \
  --only-categories=accessibility \
  --output=html \
  --output-path=./a11y-report.html \
  --chrome-flags='--headless=new'

echo ""
echo "=== Accessibility report saved to a11y-report.html ==="
echo ""
echo "Common issues to check:"
echo "  - Missing alt text on images"
echo "  - Form inputs without labels"
echo "  - Insufficient color contrast (4.5:1 minimum)"
echo "  - Missing ARIA landmarks"
echo "  - Keyboard navigation not working"

faq.question: What WCAG level should I target?
faq.answer: Target WCAG 2.1 Level AA as a minimum. It covers the most impactful requirements including color contrast, keyboard navigation, and screen reader compatibility. Level AAA is aspirational but not typically required.

faq.question: Can axe-core catch all accessibility issues?
faq.answer: No. Automated tools catch about 30-40% of WCAG issues. Manual testing with a screen reader (VoiceOver, NVDA) and keyboard-only navigation is essential to catch the rest.

faq.question: How do I fix color contrast violations?
faq.answer: Use a contrast checker tool to ensure text has at least 4.5:1 contrast ratio against its background (3:1 for large text). Chrome DevTools shows contrast ratios in the color picker.
